blockingIssues.description: Connected wallet button state renders only truncated address and omits required CASH/USDC balances (VAL-FRONTEND-004).
Swap submission path bypasses @cash/orderbook-sdk and directly submits raw wallet transactions.
Swap action is not disabled on insufficient user balance; gating is based on quote liquidity placeholder logic.
Order form submit path uses raw wallet transaction calls instead of required SDK integration.
Realtime hook set does not subscribe/handle account:{address}, so account balance updates are missing.
Network error retry UX required by feature spec is not exposed in orderbook/realtime view.
Integration test flow does not execute required on-chain deposit/place/match/withdraw operations.
Integration workflow does not verify frontend UI state reflects seeded depth/trades.

appliedUpdates.description: Added .factory/library/aptos-events-v1-deprecation.md to capture v1 events endpoint deprecation context and migration direction.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make SDK usage explicit and mandatory for order/swap/cancel transaction submission in frontend features unless a feature explicitly waives it; also add a required verification check for connected-state CASH/USDC balance rendering.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: frontend-swap-interface and frontend-orderbook-view both used direct wallet transaction payloads; frontend-scaffold-wallet missed connected balances while still reporting wallet flow complete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a frontend convention that trading transactions should go through shared SDK abstractions (SDK-first) to keep wallet payload construction centralized and consistent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Architecture references frontend→SDK→RPC flow, but milestone frontend implementations diverged and repeated direct payload construction.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that deployment/ops scripts may follow script-first implementation with focused utility tests plus service-level verification when strict test-first flow is impractical.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: deployment-integration transcript and skill feedback show repeated tension between strict tests-first guidance and operational script work.

rejectedObservations.observation: frontend-worker skill inconsistency around SDK vs direct wallet submission
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate (covered in suggestedGuidanceUpdates as a single consolidated SDK-first recommendation)

rejectedObservations.observation: WebSocket service healthcheck uses placeholder fallback command
rejectedObservations.reason: requires overwriting existing service definition; not applied in this scrutiny round under additive-only update constraints

rejectedObservations.observation: Document pre-commit scanner behavior for base64-like literals
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous and environment-dependent without a reproducible repository-level rule
